message:
refactor(unistd): hoist known_remaining lookup in read/write

Reduces the read/write tracked-buffer guard to a single
known_remaining(buf) call shared by the membrane decision call
and the EFAULT short-circuit, instead of the previous form which
asked the tracker twice (once for the decide() heuristic and
once via tracked_region_fits()).

The two original lookups always returned the same value — the
tracker is point-in-time consistent within a single function
call — so the duplicate query was wasted work. Hoisting it into
initial_remaining: Option<usize> keeps the contract identical:

* runtime_policy::decide receives initial_remaining.is_none() in
  the same position the previous code passed
  known_remaining(...).is_none(), so policy decisions are
  byte-for-byte equivalent.
* The EFAULT gate now expresses the same condition as
  tracked_region_fits — "tracker has a record AND that record
  cannot accommodate count bytes" — via
  initial_remaining.is_some_and(|remaining| count > remaining)
  inline, so the helper is no longer the source of truth on this
  path. (tracked_region_fits is still defined in io_internal_abi
  for the FILE vtable callers.)

No behavior change. Untracked pointers (initial_remaining =
None) still bypass the EFAULT gate, tracked pointers with
sufficient room still proceed, and tracked pointers shorter
than count still surface EFAULT before the syscall.
